name: ITI — Industrial Training Institute (Craftsman Training Scheme)
odia_name: ଆଇ.ଟି.ଆଇ — ଶ୍ରେଣୀ ଶ୍ରମ ସଂସ୍ଥାନ (ଶ୍ରମ ବ୍ୟବସାୟ ଶ୍ରେଣୀ)
entry_after: Class 8 pass (some trades) or Class 10 pass (most trades)
summary: 1–2 year government trade training (electrician, fitter, welder, mechanic, COPA, etc.) at ~637 ITIs across Odisha — strong feeder into apprenticeships and plant hiring (e.g., Rourkela Steel Plant).
amount_inr: Government ITI fees are low; students with family income below ~₹2.5 lakh may be exempt from admission fee (check current rules at dte.odisha.gov.in). Scholarships (Post-Matric PRERANA, Sudakshya for girls) apply.
deadline_pattern: Admissions via SAMS (samsodisha.gov.in) typically June–July each year. All India Trade Test (AITT) held twice yearly (February and August). Verify current SAMS calendar.
documents: Class 10 marksheet and certificate (or Class 8 for eligible trades)
Income certificate (if applying for fee exemption)
notes: ~637 ITIs in Odisha (government + private). Governed by SCTE&VT / DTET Odisha. Completing ITI opens the door to NAPS apprenticeships (stipend ₹6,800–₹12,300/month), SAIL RSP plant hiring, and lateral entry into B.Tech 2nd year via OJEE. All AITT pass-outs receive the National Trade Certificate (NTC). Portal for direct ITI/trade info: dtet.odisha.gov.in

name: Polytechnic — 3-Year Diploma in Engineering / Technology
entry_after: Class 10 pass (min ~35% aggregate, ~30% in Maths/Science/English; min age 14). Lateral entry to 2nd year for ITI holders or +2 Science students.
summary: 3-year diploma in engineering or technology after Class 10 — leads to Junior Engineer (JE) posts in government, OJEE lateral entry into B.Tech, and PSU jobs; ~159 diploma institutions in Odisha (35 government, ~124 private).
amount_inr: Government polytechnic fees are low. Supernumerary seats: TFW (Tuition Fee Waiver) 5% for economically weak; EWS 10% seats. Scholarships: Post-Matric PRERANA, Sudakshya (girls), Kalia/Krishi Vidya Nidhi (farmers' children), Green Passage (orphans).
deadline_pattern: SAMS e-admission (skill.samsodisha.gov.in) typically June–July. Verify current calendar at samsodisha.gov.in.
time_to_income: 3 years (diploma completion); earlier via part-time work or apprenticeship
notes: Exit paths: OSSC Junior Engineer (JE) exams; OJEE lateral entry to B.Tech 2nd year; PSU apprentice/GET intakes. ITI holders get 80% of lateral seats (20% for +2 PCM). Lateral portal: ojee.nic.in

name: NAPS — National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme
entry_after: Age 14+ (age 18+ for hazardous trades). Suitable for Class 8/10/ITI/12/Diploma holders.
summary: Earn while you train — NAPS places you inside a real company for 1–3 years with a monthly government-set stipend; over 43 lakh apprentices engaged as of May 2025; ~72% get employed after training.
amount_inr: ₹6,800–₹12,300/month stipend (revised upward 36% in May 2025, CPI-adjusted biennially): higher stipend for higher qualifications. Government reimburses employers 25% of stipend up to ₹1,500/month via DBT.
deadline_pattern: Ongoing — new openings posted continuously by employers. Search by trade, district, and qualification on apprenticeshipindia.gov.in. No fixed annual window.
documents: Educational certificate (Class 8, 10, ITI, or 12 as required by the trade)
time_to_income: Immediate — stipend starts from Day 1 of apprenticeship
notes: Stipend revised at the 38th Central Apprenticeship Council meeting (26 May 2025) from ₹5,000–₹9,000 to ₹6,800–₹12,300/month. CPI adjustment every 2 years. For diploma/degree holders the parallel scheme is NATS (Ministry of Education): nats.education.gov.in. Girls in ITI/polytechnic also qualify for Sudakshya apprenticeship support on top of NAPS stipend.

name: DDU-GKY — Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana
odia_name: DDU-GKY — ଦୀନ ଦୟାଳ ଉପାଧ୍ୟାୟ ଗ୍ରାମୀଣ କୌଶଳ ଯୋଜନା
hindi_name: DDU-GKY — दीन दयाल उपाध्याय ग्रामीण कौशल्य योजना
entry_after: Age 15–35; rural poor families; no minimum educational qualification for most trades
summary: Free placement-linked skill training (often residential with food/lodging) for rural youth aged 15–35 from poor families — Odisha (implemented by ORMAS) has been a strong national performer; training leads directly to a placed job.
amount_inr: Completely free — training, food, accommodation, uniform, kit all covered for eligible students. Post-placement tracking for at least 1 year.
deadline_pattern: Continuous intake — contact ORMAS (Odisha Rural Development & Marketing Society) or the local DDU-GKY training centre. No fixed annual window.
documents: BPL card or income certificate (rural poor)
Class 8/10 certificate (if available — not always required)
notes: Implemented in Odisha by ORMAS. Sectors covered include hospitality, retail, healthcare, IT, construction, apparel, and more. For out-of-school / dropout students this is often the most accessible formal skills route. Contact: ormas.nic.in or the District Rural Development Agency (DRDA).

name: PMKVY — Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (Free Skill Certification)
odia_name: PMKVY — ପ୍ରଧାନ ମନ୍ତ୍ରୀ କୌଶଳ ବିକାଶ ଯୋଜନା
entry_after: No minimum class requirement for most courses; age 15+
summary: Free short-term skill training and government certification (1–3 months) for any trade — also covers Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL) so workers who already have skills can get a formal certificate.
amount_inr: Free. Some trainees receive a post-assessment reward (verify current amount on portal).
deadline_pattern: Ongoing — find a PMKVY-approved training centre near you on skillindiadigital.gov.in. No fixed window.
time_to_income: 1–6 months (training) + job placement (varies by trade and centre)
notes: Also see: OSDA (Odisha Skill Development Authority) at skillodisha.gov.in for state-specific schemes. Job outcomes vary by centre and sector — frame honestly: PMKVY gives a certificate, but placement quality depends on the sector and the specific training centre. RPL is especially useful for students or workers who already practise a skill informally.

name: KVK — Krishi Vigyan Kendra Farm Skills Training (Free, District-Level)
entry_after: No minimum qualification — open to farmers, farm women, and rural youth including school dropouts
summary: Free hands-on farm training at district-level KVKs (under OUAT / ICAR) in dairy, poultry, horticulture, beekeeping, mushroom cultivation, food processing, nursery management, and fisheries — explicitly designed for rural youth and school dropouts seeking self-employment.
amount_inr: Free training. Participants may access government scheme financing (PM-KISAN, KALIA, Odisha CMEGP) afterward for their enterprise.
deadline_pattern: Trainings run throughout the year at the district KVK — contact the KVK in your district directly. No fixed annual window.
documents: Land patta or tenancy proof (if applying for farm-specific follow-up schemes)
BPL card / caste certificate (for certain scheme linkages)
time_to_income: Months to ~1 year (skills apply immediately to existing farm or new enterprise)
notes: Documented success: KVK-trained youth in Odisha have built sustainable livelihoods in horticulture (banana, watermelon), dairy, and food processing. This is the 'improve the farm we already have' path — especially strong for students whose families are already farming. Combine with Krishi Vidya Nidhi scholarship (if family is KALIA beneficiary) and ORMAS / CMEGP enterprise finance.

name: Government Jobs After Class 10 (Group C / D Posts)
hindi_name: दसवीं के बाद सरकारी नौकरी (ग्रुप C/D)
entry_after: Class 10 pass (specific mark/subject requirements vary by post)
summary: Many central and Odisha state government posts are open after Class 10 — Railways Group D, SSC MTS, India Post GDS, SSC GD Constable, state police constable, forest guard, and defence tradesman — offering stable salary, pension, and job security.
amount_inr: Starting pay approximately ₹18,000–₹25,000/month (7th Pay Commission Level 1–3); exact figure varies by post
deadline_pattern: Vacancy notifications are released throughout the year — no single fixed window. Monitor: sarkariresult.com for central jobs, odisha.gov.in and ossc.gov.in for Odisha state jobs. Exam prep typically takes 6 months–2 years.
documents: Caste certificate (if applying under reserved category)
Physical fitness certificate (for defence / police / forest guard posts)
time_to_income: 6 months–2 years from starting exam prep to first salary
notes: Key openings: India Post GDS (Grade D: merit on Class 10 marks only, no exam!); RRB Group D (railways); SSC MTS (multi-tasking staff, central government); SSC GD Constable; state police constable; forest guard. No interview in most. Category reservations give SC/ST/OBC candidates a meaningful advantage in cut-offs. Exam counts and exact eligibility change every notification — verify per vacancy.

name: Government Jobs After Class 12 (Group B / C Posts)
hindi_name: बारहवीं के बाद सरकारी नौकरी (ग्रुप B/C)
summary: SSC CHSL (Lower Division Clerk, Data Entry Operator, Postal Assistant), RRB NTPC (Junior Clerk, Ticket Collector, Station Master path), state Group C posts, banking support staff — higher starting pay than Class-10 posts, still no degree required.
amount_inr: Starting pay approximately ₹25,000–₹35,000/month (7th Pay Commission); exact figure depends on post and level
deadline_pattern: Notifications throughout the year. Monitor ssc.gov.in (SSC), indianrailways.gov.in (RRB), ossc.gov.in (Odisha state).
time_to_income: 6 months–2 years from starting exam prep to first salary
notes: SSC CHSL is a 3-tier exam (Tier 1 written, Tier 2 written + skill test, Tier 3 document verification). RRB NTPC has multiple levels — station master and junior station master require Class 12. Banking: IBPS clerk needs Class 12 + basic computer knowledge. NDA also requires Class 12 PCM (Defence, not civilian).

name: Agniveer — 4-Year Defence Service (Army / Navy / Air Force)
odia_name: ଅଗ୍ନିବୀର — ସୈନ୍ୟ ସେବା (ଥଳ / ଜଳ / ବାୟୁ ସେନା)
entry_after: Class 10 pass (General Duty, Tradesman for some posts); Class 12 PCM / specified stream (Technical, Clerk, Air Force, Navy technical trades)
summary: 4-year service in the Indian Armed Forces with monthly salary, allowances, annual training, and a Seva Nidhi corpus on exit — ~25% are retained permanently; the rest get the corpus and a preference certificate for central/state govt jobs.
deadline_pattern: Each notification specifies DOB window and physical standards — verify at joinindianarmy.nic.in, joinindiannavy.gov.in, or careerindianairforce.cdac.in. Age window is approximately 17.5–21 years (some notifications widened to 22 — verify each cycle). Physical: 1.6 km run benchmark ~5:30–5:45 min for Army GD; standards vary by branch and region.
documents: Class 10 marksheet and certificate (GD and Tradesman)
Class 12 marksheet (Technical / Clerk / Air Force / Navy technical)
Physical fitness — meet standards before applying (height, weight, chest, run time)
time_to_income: Salary from Day 1 of service (typically 6–12 months after notification)
notes: Regional physical relaxations exist for hill/tribal areas and sports persons — real advantage for many Odisha rural candidates (tribal belt, hilly districts). Army GD: Class 10 ≥45% aggregate. Class 10 marks for GD, Class 12 PCM for Technical. Selection: online Common Entrance Exam → Physical Fitness Test → Medical → Document verification. Seva Nidhi at exit: ~₹11.71 lakh (verify current amount). Preference for 25% permanent retention; remaining 75% get certificate usable for central/state govt job applications.

name: GNM — General Nursing and Midwifery (3.5-Year Diploma)
odia_name: GNM — ସାଧାରଣ ନର୍ସିଂ ଓ ଦାଇ ଡ଼ିପ୍ଲୋମା
entry_after: Class 12 (Science with Biology preferred — verify whether Arts/Commerce accepted at specific colleges; government GNM schools may require PCB)
summary: 3.5-year GNM nursing diploma from a government nursing school — strong, persistent demand for nurses in government hospitals and private healthcare; no entrance exam for government GNM admissions (merit-based counselling).
amount_inr: Government nursing schools have low/no fees. Scholarships: Post-Matric PRERANA, Sudakshya (girls), Kalia/NSKY apply. Verify stipend during internship.
deadline_pattern: Admissions via DMET Odisha counselling — 2026-27 registration was open to approximately 11 July 2026. Verify at dohodisha.nic.in.
documents: Class 12 marksheet and certificate (with Biology where required)
notes: Government GNM seats are limited; many seats are in private colleges (fees vary widely). No separate entrance exam for GNM — merit-based on Class 12 marks + DMET counselling. After GNM, nurses can upgrade to B.Sc Nursing (post-basic, 2 years). Strong job market: government hospital recruitment, AIIMS/ESIC/ECHS, and state NHM.

name: SAIL Rourkela Steel Plant (RSP) Apprenticeship — Local Odisha Path
entry_after: ITI pass (for trade apprentice) or Diploma pass (for technician apprentice) or Degree (for graduate apprentice)
summary: SAIL Rourkela Steel Plant (Sundargarh district) runs regular apprentice intakes — merit-based, no fee, no exam — with preference given first to Sundargarh district candidates then other Odisha candidates; 816 apprentices taken in the most recent large intake (2025).
amount_inr: Stipend per NAPS rates (₹6,800–₹12,300/month depending on qualification). No application or exam fee.
deadline_pattern: Intake notifications released periodically (not annually fixed). Monitor sailrsp.co.in and apprenticeshipindia.gov.in. The 2025 intake had 440 trade/ITI + 271 technician/diploma + 105 graduate apprentice slots.
documents: ITI National Trade Certificate or Diploma certificate (as applicable)
time_to_income: Immediate — stipend from Day 1 of apprenticeship
notes: Sundargarh district candidates are the first preference. Strong pipeline into direct RSP/SAIL employment for high performers. Also consider NALCO (Angul smelter, Damanjodi refinery) and Tata Steel Kalinganagar (Jajpur) for similar regional industrial apprentice paths. Coal sector (Mahanadi Coalfields, Talcher/Angul): note that coal faces long-term transition risk (some mines projected to close 2030–2040) — frame as solid now but not a 40-year career.
